changes_made: Renamed k8s_service_mesh_mtls_enabled to k8s_service_mesh_mtls_enforced for consistency with naming conventions
review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security controls related to information flow enforcement. However, the naming of 'k8s_service_mesh_mtls_enabled' was adjusted to 'k8s_service_mesh_mtls_enforced' to better align with the pattern of indicating enforcement rather than mere enablement. This change ensures consistency with the descriptive and specific nature of the function names.
naming_improvements: Ensure function names reflect enforcement rather than just enablement where applicable
title: Information Flow Enforcement - Information flow enforcement | enable and disable security or privacy policy fil
step1_reasoning: The control AC-4(10) focuses on enforcing security policies related to information flow, such as enabling or disabling security features like TLS. In Kubernetes, similar controls can be implemented through NetworkPolicies for enforcing secure communication, Ingress configurations for TLS, Service Mesh for mTLS, and Admission Controllers for enforcing security policies at the time of resource creation or modification.
